campus: Department of Computer Science (Dipartimento di Informatica), Città Universitaria / Viale Regina Elena 295 area, Rome [uncertain]
faculty: Information Engineering, Informatics and Statistics (Ingegneria dell'informazione, informatica e statistica); Department of Informatics
admission_test_type: Verification of requirements and personal preparation; CV evaluation by a Committee, optional individual interview; MoveIN pre-selection + interview for non-EU applicants
applicant_route: non-EU residing in Italy (with Italian visa / residence permit)
application_platform: MoveIN pre-selection platform for non-EU applicants needing a visa; Infostud registration + programme Google Form (Requirements Assessment Form for the Master's Degree in Cybersecurity) for all applicants; Universitaly for visa pre-enrolment
access_quota_non_eu: Per-programme non-EU contingent is set in the official Call for Applications; not extracted in this pass [uncertain].
application_round: September 2026 intake. Non-EU (visa) MoveIN pre-selection 22 December 2025 - 15 May 2026; EU/equivalent until 31 July 2026. Programme call usually published mid-July; additional January deadline for Sapienza students graduating in the December session.
places_and_foreign_contingent: Total places and non-EU contingent published in the programme Call for Applications; not extracted in this pass [uncertain].

academic_requirements.summary: Bachelor's degree (or equivalent foreign diploma) in Computer Science, Computer Engineering, Mathematics, Physics, Statistics, Telecommunication Engineering, or a related field, AND at least 44 ECTS in the listed Italian scientific-disciplinary sectors (SSDs).
academic_requirements.minimum_ects: At least 44 ECTS within SSDs including ING-INF/05, ING-INF/01, ING-INF/02, ING-INF/03, ING-INF/04, ING-IND/31, ING-IND/35, INF/01, FIS/01, MAT/02, MAT/03, MAT/05, MAT/06, MAT/08, MAT/09, SECS-S/01, SECS-P/06, SECS-P/07, SECS-P/08, SECS-P/10, IUS/01, IUS/20.
academic_requirements.note: If the 44 ECTS are not met, applicants may still apply and complete prerequisite Single Courses. A technological core is required regardless of Study Plan orientation.
academic_requirements.source: cybersecurity.uniroma1.it/admission General Requirements; MoveIN academic requirements 2026-2027

country_specific_eligibility: Foreign qualifications must be valid per Sapienza's 'Accepted qualifications by country' (2026/2027). MoveIN minimum GPA 75/100 for non-EU applicants. https://www.uniroma1.it/sites/default/files/field_file_allegati/accepted_qualifications_by_country_26mag26.pdf
curricular_integration_policy: Applicants lacking the required 44 ECTS may enrol in Single Courses to acquire missing credits before/around admission (methods and costs per Sapienza Student Regulations).
conditional_enrolment_policy: Sapienza undergraduates may use an additional mid-January deadline to enrol after obtaining the Bachelor's degree in the December session; final admission depends on degree completion.

accepted_certificates_and_scores: Cambridge English B2 First or higher (also CAE, CPE)
Trinity College London ISE II or higher
PTE General Level 3 (B2) or higher / PTE Academic
language_requirements.alternative: B2 may also be evidenced by a self-certification listing prior English training, or assessed via interview if no certificate is provided.
language_requirements.waivers: Waived for native English speakers; holders of an English-taught diploma/degree from an accredited institution in EU/EEA/Schengen, Australia, Canada, New Zealand, the UK or the US; holders of International Baccalaureate, GCSE or comparable. Fully waived for applicants from UK, US, Canada, Ireland, Australia, New Zealand.
language_requirements.source: cybersecurity.uniroma1.it/admission; MoveIN academic requirements 2026-2027; English language requirements 2026 PDF

language_submission_timing: Submitted at MoveIN pre-selection (non-EU) and/or with the programme Requirements Assessment Form / Infostud application; self-certification accepted with later verification.
italian_language_requirement: Not required for admission; programme taught entirely in English. Italian useful for daily life [uncertain].
application_deadline_eu: EU and EU-equivalent candidates (no visa) may apply until 31 July 2026 for 2026/2027. EU deadline for the requirements-assessment step is around September per the programme page; the programme call (with the Requirements Assessment Google Form deadlines) is usually published mid-July. [uncertain on exact programme-specific dates for 2026/2027]
application_deadline_non_eu: Non-EU candidates residing abroad and requiring a visa: MoveIN pre-selection 22 December 2025 - 15 May 2026; pre-accepted students must register on Universitaly by 30 June 2026.
application_fee: EUR 30 per submitted application via MoveIN (maximum 2 applications per candidate per academic year); no fee waiver. (The programme's own Infostud requirements-assessment step also references a fee; verify in the call.)
visa_preenrolment_deadline: Pre-accepted non-EU students must pre-enrol on Universitaly by 30 June 2026.
required_documents: Transcript of records (with exams and ECTS)
English language certificate or self-certification of English training
CV (assessed by the Committee: relevance of prior career, extracurricular/professional activities)
Self-declaration of Bachelor's Degree and Exams (programme template)
document_translation_authentication: Foreign documents must comply with Sapienza's 'Accepted qualifications by country' rules; legalization / Declaration of Value / CIMEA statement may be required for foreign qualifications [uncertain on programme-specific detail].

entry_exam_or_test.type: No standardized written entrance exam. Admission by verification of requirements and personal preparation: a Committee evaluates the CV and may call applicants for an individual interview to assess personal preparation. Non-EU applicants additionally pass MoveIN pre-selection.
entry_exam_or_test.evaluation_criteria: Relevance of previous academic career including Bachelor's degree; extracurricular/professional activities; knowledge of English at B2; possible interview.

test_variant_and_session: Individual interview only in special cases / at Committee discretion; no fixed public test session [uncertain].
test_score_conversion_or_threshold: MoveIN minimum GPA 75/100; English minimum IELTS 5.5 / TOEFL iBT 80; at least 44 ECTS in listed SSDs (else Single Courses). No numeric entrance-test score.

source_quotes.quote: Programme code 33516 ... Course type Master's degree ... Bachelor class LM-66 ... Access procedure Verification of requirements and personal preparation ... Faculty Ingegneria dell'informazione, informatica e statistica ... Department INFORMATICA ... Language English

Possess a Bachelor's Degree (or equivalent Diploma granted by a foreign institution) in Computer Science, Computer Engineering, Mathematics, Physics, Statistics, Telecommunication Engineering, or a related field. Having acquired at least 44 ECTS ... within the fields listed below

The CV of each prospective student will be evaluated by a Committee ... Knowledge of English - B2 level ... Interview. In special cases, the Committee reserves the right to call the student for an individual interview to assess the personal preparation
MINIMUM GPA: 75/100 ... IELTS 5.5 or higher ... TOEFL iBT 80 or higher ... Cambridge English B2 First or higher

uncertainty_notes: The task's official_program_url (corso/2024/29932/home) renders the Cybersecurity programme; the catalogue internal programme code shown on that page is 33516 and class LM-66. (The /en/course/33516 path instead renders Computer Science / code 33508, so the task URLs for the Cybersecurity and Computer Science programmes appear swapped.)

The programme admission page (cybersecurity.uniroma1.it/admission) still references the 2022/23-2023/24 call; exact 2026/2027 programme-specific deadlines for the Requirements Assessment Google Form not yet published and must be confirmed in the new call.
Per-programme non-EU access quota and total places not extracted.
Campus building inferred from Department of Informatics location; not explicitly stated on the programme page.
Document authentication (Declaration of Value / CIMEA) requirement inferred from general Sapienza policy.
Italian language requirement assumed not required (English-taught), not explicitly stated.
official_call_url points to the catalogue 'Apply' section; a programme-specific bando PDF was not located in this pass.
